title: Receipt
description: The Receipt is produced by Stage 7 (Receipt / Attestation) of the Governed Execution Protocol. It is generated for every request regardless of outcome — approved, denied, or blocked. The receipt contains cryptographic hashes linking the intent, authorization decision, and execution result into a verifiable chain. It is consumed by Stage 8 (Audit Ledger) for immutable recording and by the Governed Corpus for decision-history analysis.

receipt_id.description: Unique identifier for this receipt (UUID v4)

request_id.description: Reference to the original request registered at Stage 1 (Intake)

intent_id.description: Reference to the canonical intent produced at Stage 3 (Structured Intent)

authorization_id.description: Reference to the authorization token produced at Stage 5 (Authorization)

decision.description: Final outcome of the governed execution: ALLOW indicates execution proceeded, DENY indicates execution was blocked

action_type.description: Canonical action type from the intent ontology, copied from the canonical intent for receipt-level querying

execution_timestamp.description: UTC Unix timestamp in milliseconds when execution completed (or when denial was recorded)

result_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the execution result payload (or the denial record for denied requests)

previous_receipt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the immediately preceding receipt, forming a hash chain for tamper detection

signature.description: ECDSA-secp256k1 signature over the canonical JSON representation of this receipt (excluding the signature field itself)
